title: In an effort to create a more equitable tax system, Assemblymember Alex Lee is pushing forward his proposal for a wealth tax on the ultra-rich.
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California has more billionaires than any other state.",
    "Ultra-wealthy individuals generate their wealth from California's robust and diverse economy.",
    "The ultra-rich utilize tax-avoidance strategies that ordinary people do not have access to.",
    "The ultra-wealthy accumulate vast amounts of wealth through assets such as stocks, bonds, and real property.",
    "Capital gains taxes can be avoided by borrowing against assets without selling them.",
    "The wealthiest top 1% held one-third of all wealth in the U.S. in 2019.",
    "The richest 25 Americans pay a tiny fraction of their wealth in taxes.",
    "Some ultra-wealthy individuals can pay little to nothing in income tax, resulting in effective tax rates lower than the average American.",
    "Jeff Bezos was able to claim a tax credit of $4,000 while having a wealth of $18 billion due to investment losses.",
    "Data shows that millionaires have lower migration rates than the general population in states with higher taxes on the rich.",
    "Taxes are not the primary reason for ultra-wealthy individuals to relocate to different states.",
    "Assemblymember Lee's proposed wealth tax could generate over $20 billion in additional revenue annually.",
    "Half of the revenue from the proposed wealth tax would come from the 186 billionaires in California.",
    "The proposed tax would apply to less than 0.1% of the richest families in California.",
    "California is facing one of the largest income inequality gaps in the U.S."
  ]
}
```
